free_text: Differential cross sections of proton elastic
scattering on Be nuclei and the total cross section
of reaction 9Be(p,n)9B near the threshold.
English translation
Three surface-barrier counters
were used. Two counters were placed fixed at the
angles of 45 and 135 degr. Third counter could rotate
and was placed at an angle of 83 degr. 30 min. Neutron
counter ZnS(Ag)10B for detection of non-delayed
threshold neutrons was placed at 0 degr. angle.
Thin beryllium target without backing was placed at the
centre of scattering chamber. Target thickness was
0.3 keV for protons  with the energy of 2 MeV.
Error of absolutization
Accuracy of the proton energy measurement
FACILITY was corrected. Data in sub 002
were taken out from F0121003. Data in sub 003 were
redigitized.
Reaction was changed in sub 002. Error
description was changed.
For the angle of 45 degr. only the errors
affecting the relative course of a curve (except
absolutization error) are given in the graphic bars.
For 135 degr. angle the error affecting the relative
course of a curve was ~8.5% and for 83.5 degr. angle
it was ~3.5%.
Fig.1(b-d)
Only the errors affecting the relative
course of a curve are given in the graphic bars. They
include statistical error and uncertainty in proton
current definition.
Fig.2
